steps.title: Use the acoustic problem
steps.content: The passage explains that in the usual quartet seating arrangement, the viola’s f-holes are angled away from the audience. Those sound openings matter because the violist wants a solo to be heard clearly.

steps.title: Reject actions that miss the issue
steps.content: Choice A would make the other instruments louder, which would not help the viola solo stand out. Choice B says the violist plays at the same volume as the cellist, but volume alone does not fix the direction of the f-holes. Choice D changes where the player looks, not how the instrument projects sound.

steps.title: Point the instrument outward
steps.content: The practical fix is to change the violist’s position so the viola faces the audience more directly. That would point the carved sound openings toward listeners and make the solo clearer. The answer is C.
